Tengo suficiente contexto para generar los documentos. Voy a producirlos ahora.{"phase":"product_draft","file":"vision","content":"# Vision

Alea is the internal management platform for **Alea — Asociación Cultural de Juegos de Mesa y Rol**, a members-only gaming club. The platform replaces manual coordination (spreadsheets, WhatsApp, word-of-mouth) with a self-service digital layer that lets members find and reserve tables on their own, and gives admins real-time visibility into room usage.

## Problem

The club has 50–200 active members sharing 6 themed rooms across a finite number of tables. Without a system, reservations conflict, no-shows waste capacity, and admins spend disproportionate time on coordination that could be self-served.

## North-Star (12–24 months)

A frictionless day-to-day tool that every Alea member uses without thinking about it — reservations made in under a minute, check-ins via QR, and admins empowered with just enough reporting to run the club efficiently. This is a **single-club, single-tenant product**: the goal is depth of utility for Alea, not breadth across other associations.

## Success Looks Like

- Members self-manage reservations and profiles without contacting admins
- Admins can block rooms for events and track no-shows with zero manual work
- The platform is stable, accessible (WCAG 2.2 AA), and bilingual (es/en) for the full member base"}{"phase":"product_draft","file":"users","content":"# Users

## Persona 1 — Club Member

**Who:** An Alea association member (50–200 total). Ranges from weekly regulars to occasional visitors. Mix of ages and tech comfort levels; Spanish is the primary language for most.

**Jobs to be done:**
- Browse available tables across 6 rooms for a given time slot
- Reserve a table quickly and reliably
- Cancel a reservation when plans change (subject to 60-min cutoff)
- Check in via QR code on arrival to activate the reservation
- Manage their own profile and credentials without contacting an admin

**Pain points today:**
- No self-service password change or profile editing
- Uncertainty about whether a reservation is still active after check-in

---

## Persona 2 — Club Admin

**Who:** A small group of trusted members or board members with elevated access. Likely 2–5 people.

**Jobs to be done:**
- View and cancel any member's reservation
- Manage users (create, edit, deactivate)
- Block rooms or tables for club events
- Track no-shows and repeated offenders
- Manage equipment tied to tables

**Pain points today:**
- No room blocking for events — admins must manually communicate unavailability
- No no-show tracking — repeat offenders are handled ad hoc
- No equipment visibility tied to specific table bookings"}{"phase":"product_draft","file":"current","content":"# Current State

## Status: Pre-Launch (targeting release in 2–4 weeks)

The MVP is feature-complete and hardened. 279 tests pass. Two PRs are open on `develop` awaiting final merge before the production deploy.

## What Is Shipped and Working

| Feature | Status |
|---|---|
| Member authentication (member number or email + password) | ✅ Shipped |
| Room & table browsing with real-time availability | ✅ Shipped |
| Table reservation with overlap prevention | ✅ Shipped |
| Reservation cancellation with 60-min cutoff | ✅ Shipped |
| QR code check-in and table activation | ✅ Shipped |
| Pending reservation cancellation | ✅ Shipped (PR #83) |
| Admin dashboard (users, rooms, tables, reservations) | ✅ Shipped |
| Auto-cancel grace period (cron job) | ✅ Shipped |
| Removable-top tables with dual-surface booking | ✅ Shipped |
| i18n — Spanish (default) + English | ✅ Shipped |
| WCAG 2.2 AA accessibility baseline | ✅ Shipped |

## What Is NOT Yet Built

- Member self-service: profile editing, password change
- No-show tracking
- Room/table blocking for club events
- Equipment management
- Admin reporting and statistics
- Calendar/availability view improvements
- Locale switcher fix (KIM-360)"}Now the full proposal:{"phase":"product_proposal","drafts":"{"vision": "# Vision\n\nAlea is the internal management platform for **Alea — Asociación Cultural de Juegos de Mesa y Rol**, a members-only gaming club. The platform replaces manual coordination (spreadsheets, WhatsApp, word-of-mouth) with a self-service digital layer that lets members find and reserve tables on their own, and gives admins real-time visibility into room usage.\n\n## Problem\n\nThe club has 50–200 active members sharing 6 themed rooms across a finite number of tables. Without a system, reservations conflict, no-shows waste capacity, and admins spend disproportionate time on coordination that could be self-served.\n\n## North-Star (12–24 months)\n\nA frictionless day-to-day tool that every Alea member uses without thinking about it — reservations made in under a minute, check-ins via QR, and admins empowered with just enough reporting to run the club efficiently. This is a **single-club, single-tenant product**: the goal is depth of utility for Alea, not breadth across other associations.\n\n## Success Looks Like\n\n- Members self-manage reservations and profiles without contacting admins\n- Admins can block rooms for events and track no-shows with zero manual work\n- The platform is stable, accessible (WCAG 2.2 AA), and bilingual (es/en) for the full member base", "users": "# Users\n\n## Persona 1 — Club Member\n\n**Who:** An Alea association member (50–200 total). Ranges from weekly regulars to occasional visitors. Mix of ages and tech comfort levels; Spanish is the primary language for most.\n\n**Jobs to be done:**\n- Browse available tables across 6 rooms for a given time slot\n- Reserve a table quickly and reliably\n- Cancel a reservation when plans change (subject to 60-min cutoff)\n- Check in via QR code on arrival to activate the reservation\n- Manage their own profile and credentials without contacting an admin\n\n**Pain points today:**\n- No self-service password change or profile editing\n- Uncertainty about whether a reservation is still active after check-in\n\n---\n\n## Persona 2 — Club Admin\n\n**Who:** A small group of trusted members or board members with elevated access. Likely 2–5 people.\n\n**Jobs to be done:**\n- View and cancel any member's reservation\n- Manage users (create, edit, deactivate)\n- Block rooms or tables for club events\n- Track no-shows and repeated offenders\n- Manage equipment tied to tables\n\n**Pain points today:**\n- No room blocking for events — admins must manually communicate unavailability\n- No no-show tracking — repeat offenders are handled ad hoc\n- No equipment visibility tied to specific table bookings", "roadmap": "# Roadmap\n\n## Now — Launch Preparation (next 2–4 weeks)\n\nGoal: get the MVP into production for the Alea member base.\n\n- Merge PR #82 (cancellation cutoff UI) and PR #83 (pending reservations cancellable)\n- Fix locale switcher (KIM-360)\n- Fix missing Spanish ñ characters (KIM-361)\n- Final manual QA pass on all core flows\n- Production deploy\n\n---\n\n## Next — Member Self-Service\n\nThe highest-impact post-launch feature. Reduces admin burden and gives members full ownership of their accounts.\n\n- Profile editing (display name, contact info)\n- Password change (authenticated, in-app)\n- Member number visibility\n\n---\n\n## Later — Admin Power Features\n\nOrdered by operational impact for the club:\n\n### 1. No-Show Tracking\nRecord when a member checks in late or not at all. Expose a no-show count per member in the admin dashboard. Enables the club to enforce a fair-use policy without manual tracking.\n\n### 2. Events / Room Blocking\nAllow admins to block one or more rooms (or specific tables) for a time range, with an optional label (e.g. "Torneo de Magic"). Blocked slots appear as unavailable to members during booking.\n\n### 3. Equipment Management\nAssociate equipment items (games, expansions, accessories) with specific tables. Members can see what's available at a table when reserving.\n\n---\n\n## Backlog / Under Consideration\n\n- Admin reporting and statistics (reservation volume, peak hours, no-show rates)\n- Calendar/availability view improvements\n- 24-hour time range for reservations (KIM-317)", "current": "# Current State\n\n## Status: Pre-Launch (targeting release in 2–4 weeks)\n\nThe MVP is feature-complete and hardened. 279 tests pass. Two PRs are open on `develop` awaiting final merge before the production deploy.\n\n## What Is Shipped and Working\n\n| Feature | Status |\n|---|---|\n| Member authentication (member number or email + password) | ✅ Shipped |\n| Room & table browsing with real-time availability | ✅ Shipped |\n| Table reservation with overlap prevention | ✅ Shipped |\n| Reservation cancellation with 60-min cutoff | ✅ Shipped |\n| QR code check-in and table activation | ✅ Shipped |\n| Pending reservation cancellation | ✅ Shipped (PR #83) |\n| Admin dashboard (users, rooms, tables, reservations) | ✅ Shipped |\n| Auto-cancel grace period (cron job) | ✅ Shipped |\n| Removable-top tables with dual-surface booking | ✅ Shipped |\n| i18n — Spanish (default) + English | ✅ Shipped |\n| WCAG 2.2 AA accessibility baseline | ✅ Shipped |\n\n## What Is NOT Yet Built\n\n- Member self-service: profile editing, password change\n- No-show tracking\n- Room/table blocking for club events\n- Equipment management\n- Admin reporting and statistics\n- Calendar/availability view improvements\n- Locale switcher fix (KIM-360)", "decisions": "# Decisions\n\n## Single-tenant, club-only product\n\nAlea is built exclusively for Alea club. There is no multi-tenancy, no SaaS model, and no plans to sell or license the platform to other associations. This simplifies auth, data modeling, and compliance significantly.\n\n## Next.js 15 App Router + Supabase (migrated from NestJS)\n\nThe original backend was NestJS. The team migrated to Next.js Route Handlers + Supabase to reduce operational surface, eliminate a separate API server, and leverage Supabase's built-in auth, RLS, and realtime capabilities.\n\n## Service layer owns all privilege checks\n\nAll ownership and role validation lives in `lib/server/` (the service layer), never in route handlers. This ensures business rules are enforced consistently regardless of how a route is called.\n\n## Admin write operations use the admin Supabase client\n\n`createSupabaseServerAdminClient()` bypasses RLS for admin mutations. Regular reads use `createSupabaseServerClient()` (user-scoped). This pattern is locked in and must not be mixed.\n\n## Spanish as primary locale\n\nAll UI copy defaults to Spanish (`es`). English (`en`) is a supported secondary locale via next-intl. i18n parity between both locale files is a hard requirement — new keys must be added to both.\n\n## RPG/fantasy dark theme\n\nThe design system uses a dark RPG/fantasy aesthetic consistent with the club's identity and room names (Mirkwood, Gondolin, Khazad-dûm, Rivendell, Lothlórien, Edoras). This is intentional brand identity, not a default theme."}"}{"phase":"product_proposal","drafts":{"vision":"# Vision

Alea is the internal management platform for **Alea — Asociación Cultural de Juegos de Mesa y Rol**, a members-only gaming club. The platform replaces manual coordination (spreadsheets, WhatsApp, word-of-mouth) with a self-service digital layer that lets members find and reserve tables on their own, and gives admins real-time visibility into room usage.

## Problem

The club has 50–200 active members sharing 6 themed rooms across a finite number of tables. Without a system, reservations conflict, no-shows waste capacity, and admins spend disproportionate time on coordination that could be self-served.

## North-Star (12–24 months)

A frictionless day-to-day tool that every Alea member uses without thinking about it — reservations made in under a minute, check-ins via QR, and admins empowered with just enough reporting to run the club efficiently. This is a **single-club, single-tenant product**: the goal is depth of utility for Alea, not breadth across other associations.

## Success Looks Like

- Members self-manage reservations and profiles without contacting admins
- Admins can block rooms for events and track no-shows with zero manual work
- The platform is stable, accessible (WCAG 2.2 AA), and bilingual (es/en) for the full member base","users":"# Users

## Persona 1 — Club Member

**Who:** An Alea association member (50–200 total). Ranges from weekly regulars to occasional visitors. Mix of ages and tech comfort levels; Spanish is the primary language for most.

**Jobs to be done:**
- Browse available tables across 6 rooms for a given time slot
- Reserve a table quickly and reliably
- Cancel a reservation when plans change (subject to 60-min cutoff)
- Check in via QR code on arrival to activate the reservation
- Manage their own profile and credentials without contacting an admin

**Pain points today:**
- No self-service password change or profile editing
- Uncertainty about whether a reservation is still active after check-in

---

## Persona 2 — Club Admin

**Who:** A small group of trusted members or board members with elevated access. Likely 2–5 people.

**Jobs to be done:**
- View and cancel any member's reservation
- Manage users (create, edit, deactivate)
- Block rooms or tables for club events
- Track no-shows and repeated offenders
- Manage equipment tied to tables

**Pain points today:**
- No room blocking for events — admins must manually communicate unavailability
- No no-show tracking — repeat offenders are handled ad hoc
- No equipment visibility tied to specific table bookings","roadmap":"# Roadmap

## Now — Launch Preparation (next 2–4 weeks)

Goal: get the MVP into production for the Alea member base.

- Merge PR #82 (cancellation cutoff UI) and PR #83 (pending reservations cancellable)
- Fix locale switcher (KIM-360)
- Fix missing Spanish characters (KIM-361)
- Final manual QA pass on all core flows
- Production deploy

---

## Next — Member Self-Service

The highest-impact post-launch feature. Reduces admin burden and gives members full ownership of their accounts.

- Profile editing (display name, contact info)
- Password change (authenticated, in-app)
- Member number visibility

---

## Later — Admin Power Features

Ordered by operational impact for the club:

### 1. No-Show Tracking
Record when a member checks in late or not at all. Expose a no-show count per member in the admin dashboard. Enables the club to enforce a fair-use policy without manual tracking.

### 2. Events / Room Blocking
Allow admins to block one or more rooms for a time range with an optional label. Blocked slots appear as unavailable to members during booking.

### 3. Equipment Management
Associate equipment items with specific tables. Members can see what is available at a table when reserving.

---

## Backlog

- Admin reporting and statistics
- Calendar/availability view improvements
- 24-hour time range for reservations (KIM-317)","current":"# Current State

## Status: Pre-Launch (targeting release in 2–4 weeks)

The MVP is feature-complete and hardened. 279 tests pass. Two PRs are open on develop awaiting final merge before the production deploy.

## What Is Shipped

| Feature | Status |
|---|---|
| Member authentication | Shipped |
| Room & table browsing with availability | Shipped |
| Table reservation with overlap prevention | Shipped |
| Reservation cancellation with 60-min cutoff | Shipped |
| QR code check-in and table activation | Shipped |
| Pending reservation cancellation | Shipped (PR #83) |
| Admin dashboard | Shipped |
| Auto-cancel grace period (cron) | Shipped |
| Removable-top tables with dual-surface booking | Shipped |
| i18n Spanish + English | Shipped |
| WCAG 2.2 AA accessibility baseline | Shipped |

## What Is Not Yet Built

- Member self-service: profile editing, password change
- No-show tracking
- Room/table blocking for club events
- Equipment management
- Admin reporting and statistics
- Calendar/availability view improvements","decisions":"# Decisions

## Single-tenant, club-only product

Alea is built exclusively for Alea club. No multi-tenancy, no SaaS model, no plans to license to other associations. This simplifies auth, data modeling, and compliance.

## Next.js 15 App Router + Supabase (migrated from NestJS)

The original backend was NestJS. The team migrated to Next.js Route Handlers + Supabase to reduce operational surface, eliminate a separate API server, and leverage Supabase built-in auth, RLS, and realtime.

## Service layer owns all privilege checks

All ownership and role validation lives in lib/server/, never in route handlers. Business rules are enforced consistently regardless of how a route is called.

## Admin write operations use the admin Supabase client

createSupabaseServerAdminClient() bypasses RLS for admin mutations. Regular reads use createSupabaseServerClient() (user-scoped). This pattern is locked in and must not be mixed.

## Spanish as primary locale

All UI copy defaults to Spanish (es). English (en) is a supported secondary locale. i18n parity between both locale files is a hard requirement.

## RPG/fantasy dark theme

The design system uses a dark RPG/fantasy aesthetic consistent with the club identity and room names (Mirkwood, Gondolin, Khazad-dum, Rivendell, Lothlorien, Edoras). This is intentional brand identity."}}

Let me produce the full product documentation.{"phase":"product_proposal","drafts":"{"vision":"# Vision\n\nAlea is the internal management platform for a cultural gaming association dedicated to board games and tabletop RPGs. It replaces manual coordination (WhatsApp, spreadsheets, in-person check-ins) with a self-service web application that gives members full control over their table reservations and gives admins a single dashboard to run the club.\n\n## North-star\n\nEvery Alea member can walk into the club knowing exactly which table is available, reserve it in seconds from their phone, and check in with a QR scan — with zero admin intervention required for day-to-day operations.\n\n## Scope\n\nAlea is built exclusively for this club. It is not a multi-tenant platform and there are no plans to generalise it for other associations. Every product decision should optimise for the experience of Alea's 50–200 active members and its small admin team, not for configurability or white-labelling.\n\n## Success in 12–24 months\n\n- Members self-manage their full lifecycle: registration, reservations, profile, and password — with no admin involvement in routine operations.\n- Admins spend their time on exceptions (no-shows, room blocking for events, equipment tracking) rather than on data entry.\n- The platform handles Alea's growth to a larger member base without requiring a rewrite or infrastructure change.","users":"# Users\n\n## Primary personas\n\n### Club Member\n\n**Size:** 50–200 active members.\n\n**Job to be done:**\n- Know which tables are free before travelling to the club.\n- Reserve a specific table (or surface on a removable-top table) for a chosen time slot.\n- Cancel a reservation when plans change (subject to the 60-minute cutoff rule).\n- Check in at the table on arrival via QR code, confirming the reservation is active.\n- Manage their own account: update profile information and change their password.\n\n**Relationship with the platform:** Regular, habitual use. Members visit the club weekly or semi-weekly and are expected to interact with the reservation flow every visit once the platform is live.\n\n**Key frustrations before Alea:**\n- No visibility into table availability before arriving.\n- Manual check-in process requiring admin presence.\n- No self-service for profile or credential changes.\n\n---\n\n### Club Administrator\n\n**Size:** Small team (2–5 people).\n\n**Job to be done:**\n- Manage the member roster: create, edit, and deactivate user accounts.\n- Configure rooms and tables (capacity, removable-top setup, availability windows).\n- Cancel reservations on behalf of members when needed.\n- (Future) Block rooms or tables for events.\n- (Future) Track no-shows and take corrective action.\n- (Future) Manage club equipment inventory.\n\n**Relationship with the platform:** Daily operational use. Admins need a reliable dashboard that surfaces exceptions and lets them act quickly, not a complex reporting tool.","roadmap":"# Roadmap\n\n## Now — MVP Launch (target: next 2–4 weeks)\n\nThe core platform is feature-complete and test-passing (279 tests). The remaining work before launch:\n\n- Merge PR #82 (KIM-341): Cancellation cutoff UI — team-review clean, awaiting manual QA.\n- Merge PR #83 (KIM-362): Pending reservations cancellable.\n- Final QA pass on the end-to-end member flow (reserve → check-in → cancel).\n- Production deployment and smoke testing.\n\n**Launch criteria:** Both PRs merged, full E2E smoke pass, no P0 bugs open.\n\n---\n\n## Next — Member Self-Service (post-launch, priority 1)\n\nMembers currently cannot update their own profile or change their password without admin involvement. This is the most impactful gap between the MVP and a fully autonomous member experience.\n\n**Scope:**\n- Profile editing: update display name, email, and contact details.\n- Password change: authenticated flow with current-password confirmation.\n- Member number display (read-only).\n\n**Acceptance criteria:** A member can complete both flows without any admin action. No regression to auth hardening already in place.\n\n---\n\n## Later — Operational Epics (priority order TBD)\n\nThese epics are defined in Linear but not yet sequenced for execution. Rough priority order based on admin impact:\n\n1. **No-show tracking** (KIM-329–336) — record and act on members who reserve but do not check in. Enables enforcement policies.\n2. **Events / room blocking** (KIM-332–347) — let admins block a room or table for a scheduled event, preventing member reservations during that window.\n3. **Equipment management** (KIM-348–356) — track club equipment inventory, lending, and returns.\n\n---\n\n## Backlog (known, unsequenced)\n\n- 24-hour time range for reservations (KIM-317)\n- Locale switcher fix (KIM-360)\n- Spanish translations — missing ñ characters (KIM-361)\n- Admin reporting and stats\n- Calendar / availability view improvements","current":"# Current State\n\n## What is shipped and working\n\nAs of April 2026, the following features are complete and test-covered on the `develop` branch:\n\n### Authentication\n- Member login via member number + password or email + password.\n- Registration with strong password policy (12+ chars, letter + number + special char).\n- Session management via Supabase Auth + custom server session layer.\n- Auth hardening: CSRF protection, token refresh in middleware, admin-only RLS bypass via admin client.\n\n### Rooms & Tables\n- Browse 6 themed rooms: Mirkwood, Gondolin, Khazad-dum, Rivendell, Lothlórien, Edoras.\n- Per-room table listing with real-time availability slots.\n- Removable-top tables with dual-surface booking (top/bottom surfaces independently reservable).\n\n### Reservations\n- Create reservations for a specific table and time slot.\n- Overlap restrictions enforced at the service layer.\n- Cancel reservations (subject to 60-minute cutoff: cannot cancel within 60 min of start).\n- Pending reservations are cancellable (PR #83 in review).\n- Auto-cancel grace period via cron job.\n\n### Check-in\n- QR code–based check-in at the table.\n- Check-in hardening: TOCTOU-safe activation, PGRST116 guard, status=pending guard.\n\n### Admin Dashboard\n- Full CRUD for users, rooms, and tables.\n- Reservation cancellation on behalf of any member.\n\n### Infrastructure\n- i18n: Spanish (default) + English via next-intl.\n- WCAG 2.2 AA accessibility target.\n- 279 tests passing (Vitest + React Testing Library).\n- 85% coverage threshold enforced on auth and security modules.\n\n## What is NOT yet shipped\n- Member self-service (profile editing, password change).\n- No-show tracking.\n- Events / room blocking.\n- Equipment management.\n- Admin reporting / stats.","decisions":"# Key Decisions\n\n## Single-club tool — no multi-tenancy\n\nAlea is built exclusively for this club. The data model, RLS policies, and UI are all optimised for one association. There is no plans to generalise for multiple clubs. If that ever changes, a significant refactor of the Supabase schema and auth model would be required.\n\n## Migrated from NestJS to Next.js + Supabase\n\nThe platform started as a NestJS + separate frontend architecture and was migrated to a unified Next.js 15 App Router monolith with Supabase as the data and auth layer. This reduces operational complexity (one deployment unit) and gives the small team a simpler mental model.\n\n## Service layer owns all privilege checks\n\nAll ownership and role checks live in `lib/server/` service functions — never in route handlers. Route handlers are thin: they validate input and call services. This keeps security logic auditable and centralised.\n\n## Admin writes bypass RLS via admin client\n\n`createSupabaseServerAdminClient()` is used for all admin write operations. Regular member reads use `createSupabaseServerClient()` which respects Row Level Security. This is an explicit architectural choice — not a workaround — and must be preserved in all future service additions.\n\n## Spanish as default locale\n\nThe club operates in Spain. Spanish is the default locale; English is supported for accessibility but not actively marketed. i18n key parity between `es.json` and `en.json` is enforced.\n\n## RPG / fantasy dark theme\n\nThe design system uses a dark RPG/fantasy aesthetic (Tolkien-inspired room names, custom color tokens, fantasy typography). This is a deliberate brand choice, not a default. All new UI components must conform to the existing design tokens — no light-mode defaults."}}"}{"phase":"product_proposal","drafts":{"vision":"# Vision

Alea is the internal management platform for a cultural gaming association dedicated to board games and tabletop RPGs. It replaces manual coordination (WhatsApp, spreadsheets, in-person check-ins) with a self-service web application that gives members full control over their table reservations and gives admins a single dashboard to run the club.

## North-star

Every Alea member can walk into the club knowing exactly which table is available, reserve it in seconds from their phone, and check in with a QR scan — with zero admin intervention required for day-to-day operations.

## Scope

Alea is built exclusively for this club. It is not a multi-tenant platform and there are no plans to generalise it for other associations. Every product decision should optimise for the experience of Alea's 50–200 active members and its small admin team, not for configurability or white-labelling.

## Success in 12–24 months

- Members self-manage their full lifecycle: registration, reservations, profile, and password — with no admin involvement in routine operations.
- Admins spend their time on exceptions (no-shows, room blocking for events, equipment tracking) rather than on data entry.
- The platform handles Alea's growth to a larger member base without requiring a rewrite or infrastructure change.","users":"# Users

## Club Member

**Size:** 50–200 active members.

**Job to be done:**
- Know which tables are free before travelling to the club.
- Reserve a specific table (or surface on a removable-top table) for a chosen time slot.
- Cancel a reservation when plans change (subject to the 60-minute cutoff rule).
- Check in at the table on arrival via QR code.
- Manage their own account: update profile and change password.

**Usage pattern:** Regular, habitual. Members visit weekly or semi-weekly and are expected to use the reservation flow every visit.

## Club Administrator

**Size:** Small team (2–5 people).

**Job to be done:**
- Manage the member roster: create, edit, and deactivate accounts.
- Configure rooms and tables.
- Cancel reservations on behalf of members when needed.
- (Future) Block rooms for events, track no-shows, manage equipment.

**Usage pattern:** Daily operational use. Needs a dashboard that surfaces exceptions and enables quick action.","roadmap":"# Roadmap

## Now — MVP Launch (2–4 weeks)

The core platform is feature-complete with 279 passing tests. Remaining pre-launch work:

- Merge PR #82 (KIM-341): Cancellation cutoff UI — awaiting manual QA.
- Merge PR #83 (KIM-362): Pending reservations cancellable.
- Final E2E smoke pass on the full member flow.
- Production deployment.

**Launch criteria:** Both PRs merged, full smoke pass, no P0 bugs open.

## Next — Member Self-Service (post-launch priority 1)

Members cannot update their profile or change their password without admin help. Closing this gap is the top post-launch priority.

**Scope:**
- Profile editing: display name, email, contact details.
- Password change: authenticated flow with current-password confirmation.
- Member number display (read-only).

## Later — Operational Epics (unsequenced)

1. **No-show tracking** (KIM-329–336)
2. **Events / room blocking** (KIM-332–347)
3. **Equipment management** (KIM-348–356)

## Backlog

- 24-hour time range for reservations (KIM-317)
- Locale switcher fix (KIM-360)
- Spanish translations — missing ñ characters (KIM-361)
- Admin reporting and stats
- Calendar / availability view improvements","current":"# Current State

## Shipped and working

### Authentication
- Login via member number + password or email + password.
- Registration with strong password policy.
- Session management via Supabase Auth + custom server session layer.
- Auth hardening: CSRF, token refresh, RLS-bypassing admin client.

### Rooms & Tables
- 6 themed rooms: Mirkwood, Gondolin, Khazad-dum, Rivendell, Lothlórien, Edoras.
- Real-time availability slots per room.
- Removable-top tables with dual-surface booking.

### Reservations
- Create reservations with overlap enforcement.
- Cancel (subject to 60-min cutoff before start time).
- Pending reservations cancellable (PR #83 in review).
- Auto-cancel grace period via cron job.

### Check-in
- QR code check-in with TOCTOU-safe activation.

### Admin Dashboard
- Full CRUD for users, rooms, and tables.
- Reservation cancellation on behalf of any member.

### Infrastructure
- i18n: Spanish (default) + English.
- WCAG 2.2 AA accessibility target.
- 279 tests passing; 85% coverage threshold on auth/security modules.

## Not yet shipped
- Member self-service (profile, password).
- No-show tracking.
- Events / room blocking.
- Equipment management.
- Admin reporting / stats.","decisions":"# Key Decisions

## Single-club tool — no multi-tenancy

Alea is built for one club. The data model, RLS policies, and UI are all optimised for a single association. Multi-tenancy would require a significant schema refactor and is not planned.

## Migrated from NestJS to Next.js + Supabase

The platform migrated from a NestJS + separate frontend architecture to a unified Next.js 15 App Router monolith with Supabase. One deployment unit, simpler ops for a small team.

## Service layer owns all privilege checks

All ownership and role checks live in `lib/server/` — never in route handlers. Route handlers are thin: validate input, call services. Security logic is centralised and auditable.

## Admin writes bypass RLS via admin client

`createSupabaseServerAdminClient()` is used for all admin write operations. Regular reads use `createSupabaseServerClient()` (respects RLS). This is an explicit architectural decision, not a workaround.

## Spanish as default locale

The club operates in Spain. Spanish is the default; English is supported but secondary. i18n key parity between `es.json` and `en.json` is enforced.

## RPG / fantasy dark theme

Dark RPG/fantasy aesthetic (Tolkien-inspired rooms, custom color tokens). All new UI must conform to existing design tokens — no light-mode defaults introduced."}}Here's a summary of what went into each document:

**Vision** — Alea as a single-club internal tool. North-star: members self-serve everything, admins only handle exceptions. Scope explicitly excludes multi-tenancy.

**Users** — Two personas: the regular club member (50–200 people, weekly visitors, reservation-driven) and the small admin team (daily operational dashboard use).

**Roadmap** — Three horizons: (1) finish MVP launch in 2–4 weeks (PRs #82/#83 + smoke pass), (2) member self-service as the first post-launch epic, (3) operational epics (no-shows → room blocking → equipment) left unsequenced for now.

**Current State** — Full inventory of what's shipped: auth, 6 rooms, reservations, QR check-in, admin dashboard, i18n, 279 tests. Clear list of what's not yet shipped.

**Decisions** — Five locked-in architectural choices: single-club model, NestJS → Next.js migration, service-layer privilege checks, admin RLS bypass, Spanish default + dark RPG theme.

Would you like to save these to `aia/product/` or adjust anything before doing so?

productDrafts.vision:
# Vision

Alea is the internal management platform for **Alea — Asociación Cultural de Juegos de Mesa y Rol**, a members-only gaming club. The platform replaces manual coordination (spreadsheets, WhatsApp, word-of-mouth) with a self-service digital layer that lets members find and reserve tables on their own, and gives admins real-time visibility into room usage.

## Problem

The club has 50–200 active members sharing 6 themed rooms across a finite number of tables. Without a system, reservations conflict, no-shows waste capacity, and admins spend disproportionate time on coordination that could be self-served.

## North-Star (12–24 months)

A frictionless day-to-day tool that every Alea member uses without thinking about it — reservations made in under a minute, check-ins via QR, and admins empowered with just enough reporting to run the club efficiently. This is a **single-club, single-tenant product**: the goal is depth of utility for Alea, not breadth across other associations.

## Success Looks Like

- Members self-manage reservations and profiles without contacting admins
- Admins can block rooms for events and track no-shows with zero manual work
- The platform is stable, accessible (WCAG 2.2 AA), and bilingual (es/en) for the full member base
